name: Pain Conduit
lore:
Aesthers were once human, but a tragedy long ago caused them to lose their physical form, scattering it across the infinite planes. Those who willed their psyche back together found a world they no longer recognized, and so they left, traveling the planes in search of knowledge and hidden insights. Occasionally they return, driven by varied purposes and employing bewildering methods.

The calamity that caused the Aesthers' condition was a persistent presence on this plane for much of recorded history. It captured and tortured any who fought against it, and its prisoners—those who survived—were only released after it was destroyed. The Pain Conduits suffered worst, so infused with torment that they developed a unique ability to control pain itself. They can visit great horrors upon their foes, but they do not do so lightly. Only those who inflict harm and evil intent on others will find that same harm visited back upon themselves.
summary: A masochistic melee hybrid who turns self-inflicted pain and conditions into devastating crowd control and damage.
classNotes: Unlike other classes, you can benefit from suffering damage and negative conditions, using them in conjunction with your own abilities. Some allow you to ignore your negative conditions, including attackers adding +1 <ATTACK> when you are poisoned, or pass them to your enemies. You also have powerful healing abilities to keep you from exhausting.
Many of your abilities cause enemies to directly suffer <DAMAGE>, bypassing <SHIELD> and <WARD>. Abilities without an <ATTACK> icon are not attacks and can be performed even when you have <DISARM>.

builds.name: Melee Retaliate
builds.summary: Focuses on reflecting and retaliating damage. Plays at the front line, turning enemy aggression into true damage.
builds.strategy: The primary "thorns" build. Your goal is to be targeted by enemies and reflect that damage back. Use Reprisal and Redemption to stack retaliate effects. Remember that damage reflected through the bottom of Redemption or Reversal of Fate is true damage, bypassing shields.

builds.name: Ranged Condition
builds.summary: Stacks negative conditions to supercharge attacks or transfer them to enemies from a safe distance.
builds.strategy: Standard ranged dealer. Focus on generating Fire and Air to power up your attacks. Use Chained by Despair to load up on conditions, then dump them on high-priority targets. Wave of Anguish is your late-game AoE transfer powerhouse.

builds.name: Tank / Control
builds.summary: Utilizes high self-healing and crowd control (Disarm/Stun) to manipulate the battlefield and protect allies.
builds.strategy: A defensive variant that prioritizes Disarm and Stun. Use Chained by Despair to give yourself Disarm, then move it to an enemy to neutralize them. Your high self-healing from Wracked with Pain and Redemption keeps you in the fight.

builds.name: Support
builds.summary: Specializes in siphoning negative conditions and damage from allies to weaken and debuff foes.
builds.strategy: The ultimate utility build. Stay close to allies to pull their Poisons and Wounds with Blood Ritual. Classes with self-inflicted debuffs (like Geminate) love having you around. You can essentially make your team immune to conditions.

perkPriority.note: Essential for Self-Damage and Retaliate builds to survive reflections.

perkPriority.perk: When long resting, you may keep any negative conditions
perkPriority.note: Critical for stacking Bane, Brittle, or Poison for future turns.
Standard survivability for all front-line variants.

perkPriority.perk: Add "+1 if you have a negative condition" modifiers
perkPriority.note: The core damage boost for Ranged/Condition builds.
High value for Support and Summoning (Flesh Fiend) builds.

tips.title: Negating Damage
tips.text: If you lose a card to negate damage, you "suffer no damage." This means reflection effects will NOT trigger. Plan your health pool carefully.

tips.title: Condition Timing
tips.text: Healing removes Wound, Poison, Brittle, and Bane. Always transfer these to an enemy before you use a healing ability or a long rest.

tips.title: Refreshing Conditions
tips.text: Reapplying a condition to yourself refreshes its duration. Use this to stack Disarm or Immobilize across multiple turns.

tips.title: Curse is a Condition
tips.text: Curse is technically a negative condition when applied. Once it becomes a modifier card, it is no longer a condition.
